items.title: Ceasefire & Peacetalks, Hungary 'False Flags' & Final Rallies
items.summary: In a week that started with US President Donald Trump threatening to wipe out a whole civilisation in Iran if the regime failed to respond to his ultimatum, pesidential decorum went out of the window, with Trump unfiltered, f-ing and jeffing on Easter Sunday as he warned Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, followed up by surreal scenes on the White House lawn as he hammered home the message, sandwiched between the First Lady and the Easter Bunny to an audience of families and children.

items.title: Live: US delegation arrives in Pakistan for peace talks with Iranian officials
items.summary: A US ​government plane carrying ​top US officials landed in the ​Pakistani ‌capital Islamabad ⁠on Saturday for ‌peace talks with Iran. Washington and ​Tehran are ‌set to begin negotiations to permanently end the six-week-old Iran ​war that has killed thousands of people across ​the Middle ​East. Read our live-blog to follow the events as they unfold.

items.title: Djibouti President Ismaïl Omar Guelleh wins sixth term in power
items.summary: Djibouti’s long-serving President Ismaïl Omar Guelleh claimed a landslide election victory on Saturday, securing a sixth consecutive term in office with nearly 98 percent of the vote and extending his decades-long grip on the strategically placed Horn of Africa nation.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts splash down on Earth after record-breaking lunar flyby
items.summary: NASA celebrated a triumphant return from the Moon late Friday as four astronauts splashed down safely off the California coast, completing the first crewed lunar flyby in more than 50 years and marking a major milestone for the US space agency’s ambitions to return humans to deep space.

items.title: Former IS group child ‘fighters’ jailed in Iraq seek repatriation to France
items.summary: Three Frenchmen who were taken to Syria by their jihadist parents as children and forced to work for the Islamic State (IS) group are seeking repatriation, their lawyer said on Friday. The men are among thousands of IS group "fighters" who are being held in Iraq after being transferred from Syria earlier this year.

items.title: Orban's opponents targeted by AI-driven disinformation ahead of Hungary's elections
items.summary: The campaign for Hungary’s April 12 elections has been marred by disinformation. Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s party, Fidesz, has deployed a series of outrageous AI-generated videos to target his main rival Peter Magyar and his Tisza party, while a vast campaign orchestrated by fake accounts has emerged on TikTok and Facebook.

items.title: 'Trump harbors an unexplained sympathy for Vladimir Putin', Ukrainian analyst says
items.summary: Ukrainian leadership on April 8 hailed the two-week ceasefire reached between the U.S. and Iran but stressed it is time to pressure Russia to end its war in Ukraine as well. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Peter Zalmayev, Founder and Director of the Eurasia Democracy Initiative, says that the reluctance of Donald Trump to strongarm Russia into ending the war raises questions about the nature of the relationship between Trump and Vladimir Putin.

items.title: ‘We thought Beirut was going to collapse’: recounting the deadliest day of the war with Israel
items.summary: In a series of unannounced and unexpected attacks, Israel struck Beirut and several other parts of Lebanon without warning on Wednesday, killing more than 300 people and wounding at least 1,000. Local residents, lawyers, doctors and aid workers contacted by phone describe the “bloody” day that plunged the country into chaos.

items.title: Beirut hospitals overwhelmed, survivors recount carnage scenes
items.summary: A day after intense Israeli strikes pounded Lebanon’s capital, survivors on Thursday recounted scenes of carnage as hospitals struggled to cope with a surge of casualties. Wednesday marked the deadliest day in Lebanon in more than five weeks of renewed war between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah.

items.title: France's drone minehunters target Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: As the Strait of Hormuz remains at a standstill Iran has affirmed that a large part of the passage contains naval mines that will explode ships passing through. Very few companies are capable of de-mining seas. But, our colleagues at France 2 met a French one who has developed a mine hunting ship that uses high-performing drones to detect and destroy mines underwater.

items.title: La Chine fait planer un mystère aérien au large de Shanghai
items.summary: La Chine a instauré, fin mars, une zone de restriction aérienne sur une vaste région maritime au large de Shanghai. Elle doit rester en place pendant une période inédite de 40 jours et Pékin refuse d’expliquer pourquoi.

translation.title: China hangs an aerial mystery off Shanghai
translation.summary: At the end of March, China established an air restriction zone on a large maritime area off Shanghai. It must remain in place for an unprecedented period of 40 days and Beijing refuses to explain why.

items.title: Guerre au Moyen-Orient en direct : la délégation américaine arrivée au Pakistan pour négocier avec l'Iran
items.summary: ⁠Les États-Unis et l'Iran ⁠doivent tenir des ​négociations samedi au Pakistan afin de mettre fin au conflit qui dure depuis ​six semaines, bien que Téhéran ait posé des préconditions à l'ouverture des pourparlers. De son côté, le Liban a annoncé la tenue d'une rencontre avec Israël, mardi à Washington afin de discuter d'un cessez-le-feu. L'État hébreu refuse en revanche de discuter avec le Hezbollah. Suivez notre direct.

translation.title: War in the Middle East live: the American delegation arrived in Pakistan to negotiate with Iran
translation.summary: The United States and Iran must hold negotiations on Saturday in Pakistan to end the six-week conflict, although Tehran has laid down preconditions for the opening of the talks. For its part, Lebanon announced a meeting with Israel on Tuesday in Washington to discuss a ceasefire. The Hebrew state, however, refuses to discuss with Hezbollah. Follow our direct.

items.title: Après leur périple lunaire, les astronautes de la mission Artémis II de retour sur Terre
items.summary: Les ⁠quatre astronautes d'Artémis II sont ​revenus sur Terre vendredi soir, amerrissant dans l'océan Pacifique au large du sud de la Californie, au terme du premier vol habité autour de la ​Lune depuis plus d'un demi-siècle.

translation.title: After their lunar journey, the astronauts of the Artemis II mission back to Earth
translation.summary: The four Artemis II astronauts returned to Earth on Friday night, landing in the Pacific Ocean off southern California, at the end of the first manned flight around the Moon in more than half a century.

items.title: À Djibouti, Ismaïl Omar Guelleh réélu à un sixième mandat avec 97,81 % des voix
items.summary: Le président de Djibouti Ismaïl Omar Guelleh a été réélu, sans surprise, à 97,81 % des voix pour un sixième mandat à la tête de ce territoire stratégiquement situé dans la Corne de l'Afrique, selon les résultats officiels complets de la présidentielle publiés samedi matin.

translation.title: In Djibouti, Ismail Omar Guelleh re-elected to a sixth term with 97.81 per cent of the vote
translation.summary: Djibouti's President Ismaïl Omar Guelleh was re-elected, without surprise, at 97.81 per cent of the vote for a sixth term at the head of this strategically located territory in the Horn of Africa, according to the complete official presidential results published Saturday morning.

items.title: Record de distance, "coucher de Terre" et météorites : ce qu'il faut retenir de la mission Artémis II
items.summary: Après une dizaine de jours passés dans l'espace pour faire le tour de la Lune et s'être aventurés à plus de 406 000 km de notre planète, les quatre astronautes de la mission Artémis II font leur retour sur Terre vendredi soir. Tour d'horizon des moments forts vécus par l'équipage.

translation.title: Record distance, "set from Earth" and meteorites: what to remember from the Artemis II mission
translation.summary: After about ten days in space to tour the Moon and venture more than 406,000 km from our planet, the four astronauts of the Artemis II mission return to Earth on Friday night. Overview of the strong moments experienced by the crew.

items.title: Affaire Epstein : Melania Trump nie tout lien avec Jeffrey Epstein
items.summary: La Première dame des Etats-Unis Melania Trump nie avec véhémence tout lien avec les crimes commis par Jeffrey Epstein, un scandale que son mari, le président Donald Trump, a longtemps minimisé. Lors d'une allocution surprise à la Maison Blanche, la femme de Donald Trump a affirmé que les "mensonges" qui l'"associent à ce personnage infâme (...) doivent cesser aujourd'hui".

translation.title: Epstein case: Melania Trump denies any connection with Jeffrey Epstein
translation.summary: The First Lady of the United States Melania Trump vehemently denies any connection to the crimes committed by Jeffrey Epstein, a scandal that her husband, President Donald Trump, has long minimized. In a surprise speech at the White House, Donald Trump's wife claimed that the "messenges" that "associate him with this infamous character (...) must cease today".

items.title: Vatican : Emmanuel Macron rencontre le pape Léon XIV
items.summary: Le président français Emmanuel Macron, et son épouse Brigitte rencontrent le pape Léon XIV au Vatican. Macron s'est entretenu en privé avec le pape pendant une heure, et a échangé des cadeaux, dont le maillot de l'équipe de France de basket-ball dédicacé par l'équipe de France masculine.

translation.title: Vatican: Emmanuel Macron meets Pope Leo XIV
translation.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron and his wife Brigitte meet Pope Leo XIV in the Vatican. Macron spoke privately with the pope for an hour, and exchanged gifts, including the French basketball team jersey signed by the men's France team.

items.title: Irak : d'anciens mineurs "enrôlés" par le groupe État islamique demandent à être rapatriés en France
items.summary: Visés par des mandats de recherche dans des enquêtes antiterroristes, trois jeunes Français dénoncent leur enrôlement forcé au sein de l'organisation de l'État islamique et saisissent la justice française pour crimes de guerre. Ils sont détenus en Irak depuis leur transfert depuis la Syrie.

translation.title: Iraq: Former minors "recruited" by Islamic State Group apply to be repatriated to France
translation.summary: Targeted by search warrants in anti-terrorist investigations, three young Frenchmen denounce their forced recruitment into the Islamic State organization and seize French justice for war crimes. They have been detained in Iraq since their transfer from Syria.

items.title: L'essentiel du procès libyen en appel de Nicolas Sarkozy : "C'est à M. Guéant de s'expliquer"
items.summary: Le procès en appel de l’affaire du financement libyen de la campagne présidentielle de Nicolas Sarkozy de 2007 se tient à Paris depuis le 16 mars. Condamné en première instance à cinq ans de prison pour association de malfaiteurs, l'ex-chef de l'État avait été détenu 20 jours à l'automne. Résumé de la quatrième semaine d’audience, au cours de laquelle Nicolas Sarkozy, longuement interrogé, a notamment pris ses distances avec ses anciens collaborateurs Claude Guéant et Brice Hortefeux.

translation.title: Most of the Libyan trial on appeal of Nicolas Sarkozy: "It is up to Mr Guéant to explain himself"
translation.summary: The appeal of the Libyan financing case for Nicolas Sarkozy's 2007 presidential campaign has been held in Paris since 16 March. Sentenced at first instance to five years in prison for conspiracy, the former head of State was detained 20 days in the autumn. Summary of the fourth week of the hearing, during which Nicolas Sarkozy, long interrogated, took his distance from his former collaborators Claude Guéant and Brice Hortefeux.

items.title: Sébastien Lecornu veut accélérer sur l'électrification pour moins dépendre du pétrole ou du gaz
items.summary: Sébastien Lecornu a ⁠annoncé vendredi le quasi-doublement ​d'ici à 2030, de 5,5 à 10 milliards d'euros, des aides de l'État visant à ​encourager la consommation d'électricité en France afin de réduire la dépendance du pays aux énergies fossiles. L'effort ⁠du gouvernement portera principalement sur le logement et les transports, a expliqué le Premier ministre en disant vouloir "changer d'échelle, avec une véritable planification, sérieuse et suivie ​dans la durée".

translation.title: Sébastien Lecornu wants to accelerate on electrification to less depend on oil or gas
translation.summary: Sébastien Lecornu announced on Friday the near doubling by 2030, from €5.5 billion to €10 billion, of state aid to encourage electricity consumption in France in order to reduce the country's dependence on fossil fuels. The government's effort will focus on housing and transport, the Prime Minister said, "change of scale, with real, serious and sustained planning".

The main elements of the census include agricultural production conditions, food and food production, new quality of agricultural productivity, basics of rural development, living conditions of rural residents, etc. The standard time of the census is 2400 hours on 31 December 2026, and the data for the period is annual 2026. items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee on Energy Conservation Law Enforcement Inspection Unit that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection took the form of a combination of on-site inspections and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, typical research, thematic studies, spot-checking and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with the law; other issues in the implementation of the law; and suggestions and recommendations for changes to the energy-saving law.

Survivors recount a terrifying ordeal, singing bhajans moments before the accident. Rescue operations are ongoing, and helplines have been established to assist affected families.

items.title: 'Kal se khel sakte hain?': What Sooryavanshi asked doctors after being hit on helmet
items.summary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, at 15, is taking the world’s best bowlers to the cleaners. After taking on Mumbai Indians' Jasprit Bumrah a few days ago, he tore apart Royal Challengers Bengaluru seamers Josh Hazlewood and Bhuvneshwar Kumar as well. Sooryavanshi does not bother with reputations or resumes. The youngster bats with rare clarity and shows intent from ball one, and his personal side-arm thrower back in Tajpur, Samastipur, is not surprised at all.

items.title: FBI recovers deleted Signal chats, says report; check how to secure messages
items.summary: The FBI reportedly accessed deleted Signal messages from an iPhone by tapping into the device's notification database. Even after the app was removed, incoming message content was found stored, raising privacy concerns for secure messaging apps. While outgoing messages remained inaccessible, this discovery highlights a potential vulnerability in how notification previews are handled on iPhones.

items.title: TMC, BJP supporters clash during campaign in West Bengal’s Hooghly; FIR lodged

items.title: Artemis II crew's 10-day trip around Earth & Moon — timeline of Nasa’s historic lunar mission
items.summary: Artemis II successfully concluded with a safe splashdown, marking humanity's first crewed lunar journey in decades. The four astronauts travelled further than ever before, testing vital systems and paving the way for future Moon landings and Mars missions. This historic flight included the first woman, first Black astronaut, and first non-American on a lunar voyage.
